_meta.purpose: Phase 0 content audit — drives which projects get case-study pages, card slots, or no presence in Phase 3.

tiers.3: Flagship case study — full page under /projects/[slug]
tiers.2: Secondary — upgraded ProjectCard in the grid, no full page

projects.name: CollegeOS
projects.description: Global college discovery and application-intelligence platform. pgvector recommendation pipeline, Node.js chancing service, Python scraper infrastructure on PostgreSQL/Supabase.
projects.tier_reason: Flagship — production-scale canonical schema (8,200+ institutions), real predecessor proof (6k+ users with CollegeApp), strongest narrative arc: proof-of-problem → second swing. Differentiating content.
portfolio_accuracy_gaps: CRITICAL: Portfolio copy claims 'pgvector semantic retrieval' — README explicitly states pgvector is NOT installed; search is keyword + trigram + acronym matching today. Must correct before Phase 3.

CRITICAL: Portfolio says 'resilient Python scrapers keep deadlines fresh' — README says GitHub Actions refresh is gated (action_required at org level) and does not run automatically. Scrapers exist but aren't running.

Chancing model is trained on SIMULATED data (no real admit/reject outcomes yet) — portfolio copy implies calibrated real-data model. Must use honest framing: 'calibrated on stats-derived simulation; switches to real labels once 200 real outcomes are logged.'
Admissions stats coverage is partial (~1,600 of 8,200 colleges have acceptance rate data). Not mentioned in portfolio.
real_metrics: Canonical schema: 11 normalised tables under canonical.*
Recommendation pipeline: 4 stages (retrieval → ranking → diversification → explainability)
projects.honest_status: Architecture stable, pre-launch. pgvector and real chancing labels are the two open gaps between current state and the full vision.

projects.name: FoodSafe India
projects.description: 4-stage data pipeline (PDF OCR → NER → canonicalisation → dedup + confidence scoring) turning FSSAI/USFDA/AGMARKNET enforcement PDFs into a structured PostgreSQL database. FastAPI layer with 8 routers. Random Forest district-risk model, Bayesian supply-chain propagation graph, Benford's Law fraud detection.
projects.tier_reason: Strongest completely missing technical story on the current site. Three ML layers (Random Forest, Bayesian graph, Benford's fraud detection) on a real public-data problem. Airflow orchestration. FastAPI already live with 8 routers. The kind of data-engineering depth that a technical reviewer notices.
portfolio_accuracy_gaps: Portfolio projects.ts says 'FastAPI query layer... next' but the API is already built with 8 routers (auth, risk, user, search, fmcg, insurance, disputes, admin). Portfolio is underselling completed work.
Random Forest district risk model, Bayesian supply-chain propagation graph, and Benford's Law fraud detection are all built — none appear in current portfolio copy.
React SPA (CDN-based, no build step) is already built — not mentioned in portfolio.
real_metrics: 4 pipeline stages: extract → standardise → dedup → confidence score
8 API routers: auth, risk, user, search, fmcg, insurance, disputes, admin
3 ML models: Random Forest (district risk), Bayesian propagation graph (supply chain), Benford's Law (fraud)
projects.honest_status: Pipeline, API, and ML models built. Frontend is a single-file React SPA (CDN). Production hardening items: RLS context not yet set for app.user_id in API, needs a PostgreSQL production host.

projects.name: HoloForge
projects.description: Computational holography simulation framework. Angular-Spectrum propagation + Gerchberg-Saxton phase retrieval. Systematic degradation sweeps across 6 axes scored with physical (PSNR/SSIM) and perceptual-proxy (LPIPS-style) metrics. Working paper targeting arXiv cs.GR/eess.IV.
projects.tier_reason: Research differentiator — almost no other undergrad portfolio can show computational holography with real quantitative findings and an in-progress preprint. The InterferenceField canvas on the existing site is literally a nod to this work; the case study closes the loop. Chapter 4 of the scroll sequence is built for this.
portfolio_accuracy_gaps: Portfolio and research.ts accurately reflect the project state — no material gaps found.
Gradient-descent PyTorch retrieval is correctly marked as 'planned'. Head-tracked prototype and perceptual pilot study not yet in portfolio copy (minor — they're still future work per README).
real_metrics: 2-bit phase quantization: SSIM 0.93 (holds up far better than expected)
1 depth plane: SSIM 0.009 (multi-plane depth matters most)
projects.honest_status: Framework and degradation experiments complete. Preprint in progress — not submitted, no arXiv link yet. Gradient-descent PyTorch retrieval is the next experiment. Be explicit: 'in progress, targeting arXiv.'

projects.name: Meza
projects.description: Restaurant intelligence platform. POS transaction data → profit-ranked operational decisions: menu engineering, RevPASH, price elasticity, channel analytics, theft detection. Next.js + Express + Python ML.
projects.tier_reason: CT confirmed: Meza is the canonical repo (not RestaurantApp) and gets a full case study. In active talks with a pilot restaurant but no live data yet — case study must be honest about this. 10+ analytics modules, ARIMA forecasting, and Benford's void detection are the technical hooks.
portfolio_accuracy_gaps: projects.ts github link points to RestaurantApp — must be updated to https://github.com/ultramagnus23/Meza.
projects.ts marks hasCaseStudy: false — update to true when Phase 3 builds the case study page.
No pilot restaurant with real data yet — case study honest_status line must say 'in talks with first pilot restaurant; all results currently from uploaded CSV demo data.'
real_metrics: Analytics: RevPASH, LTV:CAC, Swiggy/Zomato reconciliation, ARIMA supply forecast, Benford's void detection
Status: core analytics running against uploaded POS data; pilot restaurant in talks
projects.honest_status: Core analytics built, running against uploaded CSV data. Pilot restaurant in talks — no live POS integration yet. Case study should say so explicitly.

projects.name: Orenth
projects.description: Real-time intra-campus navigation. WiFi triangulation + carrier geolocation base layer corrected by facial-recognition camera checkpoints. 'Navigate to a person, not a room.' TiE Global Top 25, India Mobile Congress 2023.
projects.tier_reason: Strong competition credentials (TiE Global Top 25 worldwide, IMC 2023 alongside Airtel/Jio) and a distinctive UX metaphor. No code repo means it's a narrative-and-credentials card, not a technical deep-dive. Existing case study page but no GitHub link.
real_metrics: TiE Global: Top 25 worldwide, Best Business Model
projects.honest_status: Done, shipped as a pitch/competition project. No public repo.

projects.name: CollegeApp
projects.description: AI chatbot helping students research and apply to colleges. Built in Grade 11. 6,000+ users organically. The proof-of-problem that led to CollegeOS.
projects.tier_reason: The 6,000 users is the key fact and it's already surfaced in the CollegeOS case study as the predecessor proof. Doesn't need its own card — it's the 'origin story' footnote in CollegeOS.

projects.name: Klein B
projects.description: Independent research on using Azolla ferns and Daphnia water fleas to address lake eutrophication. 60-day chemical-free biological protocol with deployment boat design and financial model.
projects.tier_reason: Distinctive non-CS independent research that shows breadth. No repo but a complete protocol + financial model. Keeps the site from reading as a purely software portfolio. Existing case study page.
projects.honest_status: Done. Research complete, no active organisation.

projects.name: NOVAmart Dashboard
projects.description: FastAPI server that executes a Jupyter notebook (papermill) and scrapes its outputs into a live executive dashboard. Single-HTML frontend.
projects.tier_reason: CT confirmed: show as a card, not a case study. Shows papermill orchestration and FastAPI — a distinct technical pattern worth surfacing in the grid.
projects.honest_status: Built and functional for its purpose.

projects.name: WaveSim-optics
projects.description: Wave-optics and digital-holography simulator. Double-slit experiment through Gerchberg-Saxton phase retrieval. The foundational predecessor to HoloForge.
projects.tier_reason: CT confirmed: show as a card, not a case study. Frame as the foundation/precursor to HoloForge — 'where the wave-optics work started.' The archived status and the HoloForge progression is itself a story worth showing.
projects.honest_status: Archived — explicitly superseded by HoloForge. Card copy should acknowledge this: 'the origin experiment; HoloForge is the active work.'

projects.name: CoupleUP
projects.description: Social matching platform — users sign up, get matched into couples by preferences, community votes, leaderboard.
projects.tier_reason: No README beyond a one-line description, no language detected. Don't show.
projects.honest_status: Unknown — no code visible in the README.

projects.name: faceclash
projects.tier_reason: Fun hack, not portfolio-grade for technical recruiting. Don't show.

phase_1_decisions.sound_notes: Ambient audio confirmed. Muted by default, explicit toggle in UI. Never autoplay.

clarifications_resolved.answer: Refers to WaveSim-optics. Show as a card (Tier 2), not a case study.
Refers to NOVAmart dashboard. Show as a card (Tier 2), not a case study.
ultramagnus23/Meza is canonical. Update github link in projects.ts from RestaurantApp to Meza.
Tier 3 — full case study. In talks with pilot restaurant but no live data yet. Case study must be honest about this.
Add ambient audio. Muted by default with visible toggle.
